channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Iran Gets Oil Trading Respite from US
item.description: The Treasury Department issued a temporary, 60-day license allowing Iran to sell some of its energy exports through August 21, a sweeping change after years of increasingly punitive economic sanctions.

item.title: Libya Reaches 1.4 MMbpd of Oil Production
item.description: Libya reached its highest daily crude production since 2013, moving closer to its goal of 1.5 MMbpd by yearend.

item.title: Wellesley confirms Carmen gas-condensate discovery in North Sea
item.description: Wellesley Petroleum and its partners have confirmed a gas and condensate discovery at the Carmen prospect in the Norwegian North Sea following the completion of appraisal well 35/10-16 S.

item.title: JERA Contracts 4 Ammonia Carriers
item.description: The integrated power utility signed time charter agreements under which fellow Japanese companies MOL and NYK will each provide 2 fuel ammonia tankers to support the Blue Point Project in Louisiana, U.S.

item.title: London Mayor: UK Tops Green Finance Rankings for Eighth Straight Year
item.description: As the City of London Corporation marks the fifth instalment of the Net Zero Delivery Summit this week, I reflect on the world we were in back in 2022. Only four years ago businesses and communities were recovering from…

item.title: Greek Energy Pulls In $26B As Europe Scrambles To Replace Russian Gas
item.description: Billions of dollars in investments are flowing into Greece’s energy sector as the country tries to establish itself as a vital energy transit hub for Central, Eastern, and Southeastern Europe as the European Union…

item.title: Var Energi Realigns Stakes offshore Norway
item.description: Var Energi signed agreements to acquire Pandion Energy's remaining interests offshore Norway, swap stakes with DNO and farm down its operated Goliat and Fenja fields.

item.title: The Oil Crisis Is Far From Over
item.description: The outline of conditions and topics for a negotiated settlement of the U.S.-Iran conflict, called a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) and signed by both sides last week, in all probability won't prevent the approaching…

item.title: Saudis Turn to Russian Fuel Oil as Iran War Saps Fossil Power Supplies
item.description: Saudi Arabia continues to snap up high volumes of Russia’s fuel oil as the Hormuz crisis has forced shut oil and gas wells and reduced domestic supply for power generation amid rising temperatures. Russia’s vessel…

item.title: The Race to Revive Venezuela’s Vast Oil Wealth Is Underway
item.description: After Washington snatched Venezuela’s authoritarian president, Nicolas Maduro, in a daring January 2026 night raid, the country’s long road to recovery began. Under the leadership of Maduro’s former vice-president…

item.title: U.S. Airlines Set To Pocket $40 Billion As Jet Fuel Prices Crash
item.description: U.S. airlines stand to save billions in dollars on jet fuel costs after the US-Iran peace deal sent oil prices sharply lower. Brent crude was trading at $79.22 per barrel at 6.05 am ET on Monday, nearly $20/bbl lower…

item.title: Russia Rations Fuel in Crimea as Ukraine Targets Oil Infrastructure
item.description: Russian-installed authorities in occupied Crimea have suspended fuel sales to private individuals and businesses amid a severe fuel shortage and a weak tourist season, as Ukrainian drone strikes continue to disrupt…

item.title: Chinese Grid Operators Resist Plans to Boost Renewables to Power AI
item.description: Grid operators are concerned that the Chinese drive to hike the share of renewable electricity powering AI would raise the risks for power firms as peak demand at data centers is difficult to forecast. Industry analysts…

item.title: The Phantom Peace Deal: What Washington and Tehran Are Really Playing At
item.description: There is no ‘deal’ yet between the U.S. and Iran, much less one including Israel, despite reports to the contrary. U.S. President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian signed an initial 14-point…

item.title: Equinor, Partners Agree Plan for Major North Sea Project
item.description: Equinor and its partners have agreed on a development concept for Ringvei Vest, an oil and gas project spanning 8 licenses on Norway's side of the North Sea.

item.title: Brent crude falls below $80 after Iran secures export waivers
item.description: International oil prices fell on 22 June, Monday, following the conclusion of talks between the US and Iran in Switzerland.

item.title: Adura submits new details to regulator for Jackdaw and Rosebank
item.description: Adura has provided further information to OPRED regarding its Jackdaw and Rosebank oil and gas projects in the North Sea.

item.title: Crimea Halts Fuel Sales after Attacks on Russian Refineries
item.description: Authorities in Russia-occupied Crimea said they were suspending fuel sales at filling stations, limiting supplies to state services amid Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian refineries.

item.title: Iran Says Stride Made with US after Talks Began
item.description: Iran said there had been 'major progress' in all-night discussions with the US, as the warring sides try to reach a peace deal within two months.

item.title: Heatwave, Hormuz Threats and Qatar Blast Push European Gas Prices Higher
item.description: Europe’s benchmark natural gas prices rose by nearly 2% on Monday morning in Amsterdam as a heatwave in Europe is raising short-term power demand and the U.S.-Iran talks continue amid conflicting messages about the…
